rationale: The citing paper explicitly uses Van Heerde, Gupta, and Wittink (2003) to interpret its own decomposition results, stating that elasticity decomposition is less managerially meaningful than unit-sales decomposition. It then uses that point to frame a concrete limitation of its own model: because purchase quantity is not modeled, it cannot perform the unit-sales decomposition advocated by the cited work. This is more than a passing background mention, but it is not a direct methodological extension.
relation_description: The citing paper draws on the cited paper's argument about the managerial relevance of unit-sales decomposition to qualify and contextualize its own elasticity-decomposition findings. It uses the cited work as an interpretive benchmark and to justify a stated limitation of the current model.
